findings.title: Unauthenticated Stripe webhook fails OPEN — anonymous cross-tenant billing mutation + payment-gate bypass (LIVE)
findings.failure_scenario: POST /webhooks/stripe is unauthenticated and, when env.STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET is unset (the current live test-mode state), skips ALL signature verification and routes the event to a tenant chosen entirely from the attacker-controlled body (metadata.tenantId / client_reference_id). Proven live against the deployed worker: (1) I signed up victim tenant ten_a01fa49f... then, with NO bearer token and NO signature, POSTed a forged charge.dispute.created carrying that tenantId — the API replied {"received":true,"applied":true,"frozen":true} and the victim's account flipped to billingState="disputed" (the tick's freeze guard then stops every send). (2) A forged checkout.session.completed with metadata.plan="scale" upgraded the same victim to plan="scale" (quota jumped to 18 domains/60 mailboxes) with no credential. So: any tenant can self-upgrade to any paid tier for free (payment-gate bypass), and anyone who learns a tenant id (returned at signup, embedded in the /checkout/simulate URL, and stored in support tickets/logs) can freeze, cancel, or mark-past-due any other tenant. Tenant ids are non-enumerable UUIDv4, and no real vendor spend moves in the current sandbox build, which caps blast radius today — but at Stripe/vendor activation this same fail-open path authorizes real plan quota + spend without payment and lets an outsider freeze a paying customer's sending. Documented in ROADMAP.md as "else test-sim accept" but the cross-tenant/auth-bypass impact is unacknowledged, and the webhook has NO legitimate caller in test mode (simulated checkout completes via GET /checkout/simulate, not this endpoint).
findings.fix: Fail closed: at webhooks.ts:15-21, when STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET is unset, reject the request (401/400) instead of accepting it — the webhook is never legitimately invoked in test-sim mode, so there is no test-mode use for the open path. Never derive/trust tenant identity from an unsigned event: the Stripe-Signature check IS the cross-tenant guard because it proves the event (and its Stripe-set metadata.tenantId) actually came from Stripe. Have the test suite either set a test webhook secret and sign fixtures, or exercise applyStripeWebhookEvent via the TenantDO RPC directly rather than through the unauthenticated HTTP surface. This must be closed before the business is declared done / any real customer onboards.

findings.title: Chargeback freeze is silently lifted by unrelated Stripe events — dispute lane defeats its own purpose
findings.failure_scenario: The D5 chargeback lane sets billing_state='disputed' to freeze a disputing tenant fast (to protect the master Stripe account). But only charge.dispute.closed(won) guards its transition with `AND billing_state='disputed'`. checkout.session.completed (billing.ts:139-149), customer.subscription.updated (:160-164), and invoice.payment_failed (:172-174) all overwrite billing_state UNCONDITIONALLY. A subscription stays 'active' at Stripe throughout an open dispute, so any customer.subscription.updated(active) event (renewal, plan/metadata change) — or a checkout.session.completed — arriving while a dispute is open resets billing_state to 'active'/'past_due', lifting the freeze and resuming sends during an open chargeback. Confirmed live: after a forged charge.dispute.created set billingState=disputed, a following checkout.session.completed flipped billingState back to 'active'. This is reachable with a properly signed event too (independent of the auth issue above), so it survives fixing finding #1.
findings.fix: Treat 'disputed' as a sticky state that only charge.dispute.closed may exit. Guard the checkout.session.completed / customer.subscription.updated / invoice.payment_failed transitions so they do not overwrite billing_state when it is currently 'disputed' (e.g. add `AND billing_state != 'disputed'` to those UPDATEs, or model billing_state as an explicit state machine). Add a test that FAILS on current code: dispute.created -> subscription.updated(active) must leave the tenant frozen.

findings.title: Request-body size cap bypassed on /webhooks/stripe (unauthenticated) and /cancel
findings.failure_scenario: Panel-02 established the rule that every route must reject an over-cap body via a Content-Length pre-check BEFORE materializing it (validate.ts parseJsonBody, and mcp.ts which checks content-length at :26). Two endpoints added after panel-02 bypass that: routes/webhooks.ts:13 and routes/lifecycle.ts:13 both call `await c.req.text()` with no cap. /webhooks/stripe is the worse case — it is unauthenticated AND unthrottled (no rate limiter), so an anonymous attacker can stream arbitrarily large bodies that the worker buffers fully into memory before any validation, the exact CPU/memory amplifier class panel-02 closed on /signup. /cancel is authed but any low-tier tenant can hammer it with large bodies (cancel is idempotent and voluntary cancel does NOT lock the token, so the tenant token stays active afterward).
findings.fix: Add a Content-Length guard (reject 413 over SMALL_BODY_MAX_BYTES, mirroring validate.ts / mcp.ts) before reading the body in both webhooks.ts:13 and lifecycle.ts:13. Since webhooks needs the RAW text for signature verification, cap first then read text(). Consider a global Hono bodyLimit middleware in index.ts so future raw-body routes inherit the cap by default.

findings.title: New authed money/lifecycle endpoints have no per-tenant throttle; /checkout grows unbounded self-storage
findings.failure_scenario: /checkout, /cancel (and the unauthenticated /webhooks/stripe) have no rate limiting, unlike /signup (RateLimiterDO) and /demo/run (per-tenant throttle + lifetime cap added in panel-02). startCheckout (engine/billing.ts:38-46) INSERTs a new checkout_sessions row on every call with no bound, so a single authed demo/free tenant can loop POST /checkout to grow its own DO SQLite storage indefinitely (platform-billed storage), the same abuse-cost class /demo/run was hardened against. No cross-tenant impact, but an uncapped self-amplifier on a newly added endpoint.
findings.fix: Reuse the /demo/run pattern: cap pending checkout_sessions per tenant (e.g. reuse an existing 'pending' session for the same plan instead of inserting a new row each call, or evict/limit pending sessions), and add a light per-tenant throttle to /checkout and /cancel. Rate-limit /webhooks/stripe by source as part of the finding #3 fix.
